results.claim: OP-001 selected with status FAILED, reason: kernel speedup vanishes under DRAM-bound conditions

E2E results: BS=8 -0.052%, BS=32 +0.066%, both within noise and below kill criteria
OP-002 FAILED: fused kernel slower cold-cache, all BS regressed
OP-002 overall determination is FAIL; all batch sizes regressed
E2E sweep all regressed: BS=1 0.973x, BS=8 0.989x, BS=32 0.990x
Champion-3 scored 4.90, below 5.0 minimum threshold, FAIL
Champion-3 feasibility evidence 4/10: no register measurement (est 200 vs actual 227), no prototype
Stage timestamps from state.json recording baseline through campaign_eval

OP-001 validator ran independent tests: batch sizes [1,8,32], atol=0.01, rtol=0.01, bit-identical to cuBLAS
OP-002 validator: 26/26 correctness tests passed including CUDA graph, multi-step, adversarial
OP-001 kill criteria: >=2.5% at BS=32, >=1.0% at BS=8, no regression at BS=1
OP-001 kill criteria FAIL: BS=32 +0.066% vs +2.5% required; BS=8 -0.052% vs +1.0% required
OP-002 kill criteria: all FAIL, BS=32 -1.02% vs +0.6% required, BS=1 -2.66% vs -0.5% required
Integration final decision: No candidates passed, Round EXHAUSTED
Overall score 3.56, without transcript 2.82
Cumulative speedup 1.0x, campaign status campaign_exhausted, 1 round completed
Baseline E2E latency: BS=1 6.4935s, BS=8 7.7388s, BS=32 10.2237s with <0.2% stdev
Champion-1 projected 3.4% E2E at BS=32 from 1.107x isolated kernel speedup
Severe Amdahl violation ratio < 0.01; standalone benchmarks measured L2-warm performance not production DRAM-bound
Actual E2E: BS=32 +0.066%, BS=8 -0.052%, both noise-level
OP-001 verdict: FAIL -- 5-19% kernel-level speedup does not translate to E2E; both equally DRAM-bound at ~640 GB/s
Root cause: inter-program race condition on shared Q,K conv state forced Python-level scatter workaround whose overhead exceeds savings
OP-001 files: new triton_gemm_gate_up.py, modified utils.py, linear.py, envs.py
OP-002 files: new fused_gdn_decode.py, modified qwen3_next.py
